questions.question: The Haitian Revolution (1791-1804) is notable because it established Haiti as the first Black republic in the world and the first successful slave revolt. Which leader helped win Haitian independence?
questions.explanation: Toussaint Louverture led the Haitian Revolution until he was captured by Napoleon in 1802. Jean-Jacques Dessalines completed the revolution and declared independence on January 1, 1804. Haiti (then Saint-Domingue) was France's wealthiest colony, producing 40% of Europe's sugar. The revolution sent shockwaves through slave-holding nations worldwide.
questions.memory_hook: Haiti 1804: first Black republic, first successful slave revolt. Toussaint led it (captured 1802); Dessalines declared independence.

questions.question: What phenomenon explains why a compass needle points north, due to Earth's internal structure?
options: Gravitational pull of the North Pole
questions.explanation: Earth's magnetic field is generated by the movement of molten iron in the outer core — a process called the geodynamo. The magnetic north pole does not coincide with the geographic North Pole and moves gradually. The field also protects Earth from harmful solar radiation. Magnetic field reversals (when north and south switch) have happened hundreds of times in Earth's history.
questions.memory_hook: Earth's magnetic field: generated by molten iron outer core. Protects from solar radiation. Poles reverse periodically.

questions.question: Which country won the first-ever Soccer/Football World Cup in 1930, held in Uruguay?
questions.explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final in Montevideo. Uruguay had been Olympic champions in 1924 and 1928. Only 13 nations competed, as European teams largely refused to make the long boat trip to South America. Uruguay also won the 1950 World Cup in a famous upset over Brazil.
questions.memory_hook: Uruguay: won first World Cup 1930 (hosting) and 1950 (upset Brazil). Argentina lost 4-2 in 1930 final.

questions.question: Which 1970 Francis Ford Coppola film follows the misadventures of a group of young people in 1962 California and launched the careers of Richard Dreyfuss and Harrison Ford?
questions.explanation: American Graffiti (1973 — not 1970) was George Lucas's film, not Coppola's. Coppola produced it. It launched careers of Richard Dreyfuss, Harrison Ford, Ron Howard, and Cindy Williams. The film was set in 1962 to its $775,000 budget film that earned $200 million. It inspired TV's Happy Days.
questions.memory_hook: American Graffiti 1973: George Lucas (Coppola produced). $775K budget, $200M gross. Launched Ford, Dreyfuss, Ron Howard.

questions.question: Which river, flowing through Egypt and Sudan, is divided into two branches before it reaches the Mediterranean Sea, creating a fertile delta?
questions.explanation: The Nile Delta is formed by the Rosetta (Rashid) and Damietta branches splitting north of Cairo. The delta covers about 240 km of Mediterranean coastline and is home to nearly half of Egypt's population. In ancient times the delta had seven branches — most have silted up. The fertile black soil (from annual Nile floods) enabled Egyptian civilization.
questions.memory_hook: Nile Delta: Rosetta and Damietta branches. Half of Egypt's population lives in the delta. Ancient black soil enabled civilization.

questions.question: The 1848 Communist Manifesto was written by Karl Marx and which co-author?
questions.explanation: The Communist Manifesto was commissioned by the Communist League and written in Brussels by Marx and Engels in 1847-48. It was published in London on February 21, 1848, days before revolutions swept Europe. Its opening line: 'A spectre is haunting Europe — the spectre of communism.' Engels financially supported Marx throughout his life.
questions.memory_hook: Communist Manifesto 1848: Marx + Engels. Published Feb 21 1848. Engels financially supported Marx his whole life.

questions.question: Which Disney princess, from a 1989 animated film, dreams of living on land and trades her voice to a sea witch to become human?
questions.explanation: The Little Mermaid (1989) is credited with reviving Disney animation after years of declining quality and is often called the beginning of the 'Disney Renaissance.' Ariel trades her voice to Ursula for legs. The film's score by Alan Menken and Howard Ashman won two Oscars. Jodi Benson voiced Ariel; Halle Bailey played her in the 2023 live-action remake.
questions.memory_hook: Ariel: The Little Mermaid 1989. Began Disney Renaissance. Trades voice for legs. Menken/Ashman won 2 Oscars.

questions.question: What is the name of the evolutionary adaptation where unrelated species develop similar traits due to living in similar environments?
questions.explanation: Convergent evolution explains why dolphins (mammals) and sharks (fish) look similar — both evolved streamlined bodies for fast underwater movement independently. Wings evolved independently in birds, bats, and insects. Eyes evolved independently at least 40 times across animal lineages. It's evidence that natural selection finds similar solutions to similar problems.
questions.memory_hook: Convergent evolution: unrelated species look similar due to similar environments. Dolphins and sharks, birds and bats.

questions.question: Which canal, completed in 1914, connects the Atlantic and Pacific oceans through a 77 km stretch of Central America?
questions.explanation: The Panama Canal took 10 years to build (1904-1914) and cost approximately $375 million (plus 5,600 lives in the US construction phase, after tens of thousands died in a failed French attempt). The canal uses a system of locks to raise and lower ships. The expanded canal (2016) can now accommodate 'Neopanamax' ships too large for the original locks.
questions.memory_hook: Panama Canal: built 1904-14, $375M, 5,600 deaths. Expanded 2016 for mega-ships. 77 km of locks.

questions.question: Which country's civil war (1936-1939) served as a rehearsal for World War II, with Germany and Italy supporting Francisco Franco's Nationalists?
questions.explanation: The Spanish Civil War pitted the Republican government (supported by Soviet Union and International Brigades of foreign volunteers) against Franco's Nationalist movement (backed by Hitler and Mussolini). It was the first major test of Nazi air power, particularly the bombing of the Basque town of Guernica (immortalized in Picasso's painting). Franco won and ruled Spain until 1975.
questions.memory_hook: Spanish Civil War 1936-39: Franco (Hitler + Mussolini) vs. Republic (Soviets). Guernica bombing: Picasso's response.

questions.question: Which British singer-songwriter penned 'Tiny Dancer,' 'Rocket Man,' and 'Crocodile Rock' while his lyricist partner Bernie Taupin wrote the words?
questions.explanation: Elton John (born Reginald Dwight) and Bernie Taupin are one of pop music's most successful songwriting partnerships. Taupin writes lyrics, sends them to Elton, who sets them to music — often in under an hour. Elton John has sold over 300 million records. He holds the record for fastest-selling single (Candle in the Wind 1997 at Princess Diana's funeral: 33 million copies in 37 days).
questions.memory_hook: Elton John + Bernie Taupin: Taupin writes words, Elton sets music. 300M records. 'Candle in the Wind' 1997 = fastest-selling single ever.

questions.question: In swimming, what is the correct stroke order in an individual medley race?
questions.explanation: The individual medley (IM) order is always: butterfly, backstroke, breaststroke, freestyle — or 'fly-back-breast-free.' This differs from the medley relay order (backstroke, breaststroke, butterfly, freestyle). Michael Phelps is the most decorated Olympic swimmer in the IM events.
questions.memory_hook: Individual medley order: Fly → Back → Breast → Free. Medley relay reverses the first two (Back first).
